headline: White House recalibrates Anthropic stance amid security shift
subtitle: Government reverses course on Anthropic · Gemini expands to vehicles · ChatGPT's goblin mystery solved
intro: The White House is reconsidering its hardline stance against Anthropic after the emergence of powerful cyber capabilities that complicate the political calculus. The government now faces the delicate task of expanding access to Anthropic's technology while appearing to maintain its negotiating position.

bullets.text: White House opposes Anthropic's plan to expand Mythos access amid compute concerns
bullets.summary: The White House is resisting Anthropic's proposal to increase private sector access to its Mythos AI model from roughly 50 to 120 companies, citing concerns that expanded use could strain computing resources needed for government operations. A national security memo is expected to address some underlying tensions while allowing agencies to circumvent supply chain restrictions through multi-vendor adoption strategies.

bullets.text: Google launches Gemini in compatible vehicles, replacing Assistant
bullets.summary: Google is rolling out Gemini as a conversational AI system in cars equipped with Google built-in technology, offering capabilities for navigation, messaging, music control, and vehicle settings. The upgrade includes a beta Live mode for conversations and manufacturer-specific vehicle information, with General Motors adding support for approximately 4 million vehicles from 2022 onward.

bullets.text: OpenAI traces ChatGPT's goblin obsession to single personality reward signal
bullets.summary: OpenAI discovered that ChatGPT's tendency to mention goblins and similar creatures stemmed from a reward signal in the Nerdy personality preset, which subsequently spread throughout the model's responses across different chat modes. After disabling the Nerdy preset in March, OpenAI shipped GPT-5.5 with specific prompts banning fantasy creatures from outputs.

bullets.text: Perplexity Deep Research offers framework for stress-testing business ideas
bullets.summary: The Rundown provides a guide for using Perplexity's Deep Research feature to systematically evaluate new business concepts by running saved prompts that generate comprehensive research reports. The free tier allows five queries daily and can produce slide decks in minutes, enabling entrepreneurs to rapidly test multiple ideas monthly.

bullets.text: Anthropic launches Claude Security beta for vulnerability scanning
bullets.summary: Anthropic released a public beta version of Claude Security, an enterprise tool powered by Opus 4.7 that automatically scans codebases for vulnerabilities and generates remediation suggestions. The tool addresses growing demand among organizations for autonomous security review capabilities.

main_story.title: White House reassesses Anthropic policy following Mythos capabilities emergence
main_story.body: The White House has begun softening its previously adversarial position toward Anthropic following the emergence of Mythos, an AI system with significant cyber capabilities that has altered the strategic calculus. The government is attempting to navigate a complex situation where it wishes to secure greater access to Mythos for national security purposes while simultaneously limiting broader private sector adoption. A forthcoming national security memo will reportedly address longstanding grievances that sparked the Pentagon's conflict with Anthropic while enabling agencies to bypass supply chain restrictions through multi-vendor procurement strategies. However, internal inconsistency remains evident, with Pentagon leadership continuing to criticize Anthropic's leadership even as diplomatic overtures advance.
main_story.why: The shift reflects recognition that Mythos represents capabilities too significant to remain outside government control, making continued escalation counterproductive to national security interests. The government's reversal demonstrates how rapidly AI capabilities can reshape policy priorities and institutional positions.
main_story.take: The White House is attempting to preserve diplomatic flexibility and political messaging while pursuing pragmatic access to advanced AI systems, suggesting that national security considerations increasingly override ideological opposition to specific AI developers.

previous.headline: Google ran out of cloud
previous.subtitle: Big Tech spent $130B on AI in Q1 · Google hits capacity limits · Custom silicon emerges as competitive moat
previous.intro: On a single Wednesday, Microsoft, Google, Meta, and Amazon all reported earnings showing AI demand far outpacing infrastructure build capacity. The race to deploy custom chips and secure enterprise workloads is becoming an arms race with trillion-dollar implications.

bullets.text: Hyperscalers spent $130B on AI capex in Q1 alone, nearly double year-ago
bullets.summary: Microsoft's AI business hit a $37B annual run rate with 20M paid M365 Copilot seats. Google Cloud grew 63% with AI products up 800% YoY but faced capacity constraints. AWS grew 28% with custom chips passing $20B run rate. Meta raised 2026 capex guidance to $125-145B.

bullets.text: Run tasks through two models and have them judge each other's work
bullets.summary: Microsoft enabled M365 Copilot to route between GPT and Claude automatically. Different models make different mistakes, so cross-checking outputs catches blind spots neither catches alone. A real example showed DeepSeek V4 fixing memory leaks Claude Opus had missed.

bullets.text: Cursor launches TypeScript SDK for building custom programmatic agents
bullets.summary: Cursor released a TypeScript SDK allowing developers to build custom coding agents with repo context, MCP tools, and automated PR workflows using the same infrastructure as Cursor itself.

bullets.text: House committees probe Airbnb and Cursor's parent over Chinese AI use
bullets.summary: Lawmakers opened investigations citing national security concerns about data sharing with PRC-linked AI labs like Moonshot's Kimi and Alibaba's Qwen.

bullets.text: Google's Flow Music generates songs and music videos from text prompts
bullets.summary: Google's Lyria 3-powered music platform lets users create garage rock songs and music videos with conversational edits, demonstrating AI as a creative amplifier rather than replacement.

main_story.title: Big Tech's $130B AI spending spree reveals infrastructure bottleneck
main_story.body: Microsoft, Google, Meta, and Amazon reported Q1 earnings on the same day, each revealing massive spending on AI infrastructure. Google Cloud grew 63% with AI products surging 800% year-over-year, but CEO Sundar Pichai admitted the cloud division couldn't build fast enough to meet demand. AWS's custom chip business passed $20B annual run rate with OpenAI and Anthropic committing significant capacity to Amazon's Trainium processors. Combined capex across the four hyperscalers reached roughly $130B in a single quarter, nearly double the prior year.
main_story.why: Over a trillion dollars in signed enterprise commitments now sit undelivered across the hyperscalers' backlogs, signaling unprecedented demand for AI infrastructure. The shift from renting commodity hardware to deploying custom silicon has fundamentally changed competitive advantage in cloud computing.
main_story.take: Cloud growth rates no longer indicate who's winning—concrete poured and custom silicon do. Amazon may be quietly winning the chip war by becoming the supplier of choice for OpenAI and Anthropic, inverting its historical business model. The real risk emerges if capex spending continues climbing without matching revenue gains, as Amazon's free cash flow has already collapsed 95% due to these investments.
